findings: Code+math verified against the canonical FLA reference (fla.layers.GatedDeltaNet, fla.ops.gated_delta_rule). Recurrence ordering in answer.md naive_recurrent_gated_delta_rule is correct (decays S before computing v_old, matching FLA naive.py h=h*exp(g) then v_old=h@k). Chunkwise form folds the scalar decay into the chunk-local cumsum exactly as FLA naive_chunk_gated_delta_rule; numerically chunk==recurrent to 1e-7. alpha=1 recovers DeltaNet correctly. Scaffold edit is faithful: GatedDeltaNet(hidden_size=1024, head_dim=128, num_heads=6, expand_v=2.0, use_gate=True, use_short_conv=True, conv_size=4) uses only existing constructor kwargs, 6*128=768=0.75*1024, 128*2.0=256 passes the integer value-head assertion. Two prose precision notes raised and fixed: (1) the beta=0 limit is the pure scalar-gated decay skeleton S_t=alpha_t S_{t-1} (the gating part shared with Mamba2/GLA), not the full Mamba2 layer which adds its own write back; (2) the math uses the v_t k_t^T / S_t q_t transpose convention shared with the sibling deltanet/gla/retnet traces while the code uses FLA's key-by-value orientation (intentional, consistent across the method set).
